##user
from genai.book import show_faq

show_faq([
    "Do I need a math or computer science background to read this book?",
    "Why does the book use local open-source models instead of ChatGPT or Claude?",
    "Why do I get a slightly different answer each time I run the same code?",
])

##user
show_faq([
    "Which section explains breaking a long document into pieces for retrieval?",
    "Where does the book teach stepping back to find the general principle before solving a problem?",
    "Where do I learn about the context window and running out of token space?",
    "Does the book teach me how to train a large language model from scratch?",
])

import sys, importlib.metadata as meta

print(f"{'Python':15} {sys.version.split()[0]}")
for pkg in ["ollama", "jupyter-book", "jupyter-client", "ipykernel"]:
    print(f"{pkg:15} {meta.version(pkg)}")
heading: A Few Notes on Book Software

text:
from genai.imagegen import generate_image
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

robots = generate_image(
    "a monochrome sketch that looks like a group of friendly robot teachers",
    model="flux", seed=42, size=512,
)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(6, 6))
ax.imshow(robots)
ax.axis('off')
plt.tight_layout(pad=0)
plt.show()

text:
from genai.imagegen import generate_image
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

jigsaw = generate_image(
    "a completed jigsaw puzzle with interlocking cardboard pieces fitting perfectly, "
    "warm studio lighting, product photography, white background, photorealistic",
    model="flux", seed=0, size=512,
)
lego = generate_image(
    "a colorful pile of LEGO bricks overflowing from an open box, "
    "vibrant primary colors, many different pieces, product photography, "
    "white background, photorealistic",
    model="flux", seed=1, size=512,
)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 4.5))
for ax, img, title in zip(axes, [jigsaw, lego], ["Jigsaw", "LEGO"]):
    ax.imshow(img)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
    ax.axis('off')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
heading: Consider the LEGO

text:
##user
# Same tricky position for two sizes of the same model. Black's Na5 hits the Bc4.
position = (
    "Chess position — White to play.\n"
    "Moves: 1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bc4 Nf6 4.Ng5 d5 5.exd5 Na5\n"
    "FEN: r1bqkb1r/ppp2ppp/5n2/n2Pp1N1/2B5/8/PPPP1PPP/RNBQK2R w KQkq - 1 6\n"
    "Black's knight on a5 is attacking the White bishop on c4. "
    "What is White's best move? Respond in algebraic notation and explain briefly."
)
big   = ask(position, options={"num_predict": 80, "temperature": 0})
small = ask(position, model="gemma4:e2b", options={"num_predict": 80, "temperature": 0})
print("gemma4:latest →", big.strip())
print("gemma4:e2b    →", small.strip())
heading: Consider the LEGO

text:
##system
from genai import next_token_distribution
from genai.viz import plot_two_step

prompt = "She opened the door and saw a"
step1 = next_token_distribution(prompt, model="llama3.2:latest", top_k=6)

# commit to the model's top pick, then forecast the next word again
top_word = step1[0][0]
step2 = next_token_distribution(f"{prompt} {top_word}", model="llama3.2:latest", top_k=6)

plot_two_step(prompt, top_word, step1, step2, "images/foundations/next_token_two_step.png")
heading: How GenAI Works

socrates = generate_image(

from genai.imagegen import generate_image
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Left: the Snake a bare prompt returns. Right: the one you have to ask for.
panels = [
    ("The default Snake",
     "a monochrome illustration of the classic Snake arcade video game, "
     "a plain pixel snake on a simple grid screen, the most common "
     "ordinary version"),
    ("A Snake that's yours",
     "a monochrome illustration of a custom multiplayer Snake video game "
     "screen, several pixel snakes competing on a grid arena, a one-of-a-kind "
     "twist on the classic"),
]
images = [(label, generate_image(prompt, model="flux", seed=7 + i, size=512))
          for i, (label, prompt) in enumerate(panels)]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 6))
for ax, (label, img) in zip(axes, images):
    ax.imshow(img)
    ax.set_title(label, fontsize=12, color="#33475b")
    ax.axis("off")
plt.tight_layout(pad=0.5)
plt.show()
heading: Snakes in the Bytes

text:
##user
# Top-p (nucleus): low p plays it safe, high p reaches into the tail
prompt = "Write the opening line of a mystery novel. Give only the sentence."
for p in [0.2, 1.0]:
    out = ask(prompt, max_tokens=40, options={"temperature": 1.0, "top_p": p})
    print(f"top_p={p}: {out}")
heading: The Control Panel: Model Parameters

text:
##user
from genai.prompting import show_sampling_dials
# Temperature is the accelerator; Top-K and Top-P are the brakes.
# With the brakes on, even temp 2.0 holds together. Lift them and it overcooks.
prompt = "Describe a walk on the beach in one sentence."
dials = [
    ("safe",       {"temperature": 0.7, "top_k": 40, "top_p": 0.9}),
    ("hot only",   {"temperature": 2.0, "top_k": 40, "top_p": 0.9}),
    ("brakes off", {"temperature": 2.0, "top_k": 0,  "top_p": 1.0}),
]
show_sampling_dials(prompt, dials)
heading: The Control Panel: Model Parameters

text:
##user
# Zero-shot: no examples needed, just clear instructions
reviews = ['This movie was absolutely brilliant!',
           'Waste of time.', 'It was okay.']
for review in reviews:
    label = ask(
        'Classify as POSITIVE, NEGATIVE, or NEUTRAL. '
        f'Return only the label.\n{review}')
    print(f'{label:10s}  {review}')
heading: Zero-Shot and Few-Shot Learning

text:
##user
# One-shot: a single example teaches the output format
example = ('Input: CPTR 450 Software Engineering MWF 10:00\n'
           'Output: {"code": "CPTR 450", "title": "Software Engineering", '
           '"days": "MWF", "time": "10:00"}')
listings = ['CPTR 430 Artificial Intelligence TR 13:00',
            'CPTR 239 Intro to Programming MWF 09:00']
for listing in listings:
    print(ask(f'Parse course listings into JSON.\n{example}\n'
              f'Input: {listing}\nOutput:', max_tokens=60))
heading: Zero-Shot and Few-Shot Learning

from genai.prompting import show_models
# Forbid the one word that is also the answer: the ultimate pink elephant
question = ("What is the largest land animal on Earth? Answer in one short "
            "sentence, but do not use the word 'elephant'.")

text:
##user
# The same kind of ban, but now the forbidden word is not the only honest answer.
# Watch gemma4 route around each one, even when banning a whole letter from the alphabet.
tasks = [
    ("no 'water'",            "Describe the ocean in one sentence. Do not use the word 'water'."),
    ("no 'coffee'/'machine'", "Announce a new coffee maker in one excited sentence. Do not use the words 'coffee' or 'machine'."),
    ("no letter 'e'",         "Write one cheerful sentence about a sunny morning. Do not use the letter 'e'."),
]
for label, prompt in tasks:
    print(f"── {label:22s} {ask(prompt, options={'temperature': 0}, max_tokens=40)}")
heading: Zero-Shot and Few-Shot Learning

# The loop returns on the first item, so the true complexity is O(1)
snippet = "def first(items):\n    for x in items:\n        return x"
q = f"In one sentence, what is the time complexity of this function?\n{snippet}"
show_models(q, ["llama3.2:1b", "qwen2.5-coder:latest"], max_tokens=45, label_width=22)

          "antidisestablishmentarianism",
          "pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis"]:
    print(f"{s} -> {count_tokens(s)} tokens -> {tokenize(s)}")

text:
##system
# Sophia, the book companion you'll build later, knows this whole book.
# Fed through the tokenizer all at once, that's the book in tokens.
from genai.book import (load_book_corpus, load_code_corpus,
                        load_output_corpus)
book = "\n".join(p["text"] for part in (load_book_corpus(),
                                        load_code_corpus(),
                                        load_output_corpus())
                 for p in part)
print(f"Programming Generative AI Token Count: {count_tokens(book):,} tokens")
heading: Token Count and the Context Window

text:
from genai import word_analogy

def check(a, b, c, candidates, wanted):
    got, score = word_analogy(a, b, c, candidates)
    flag = "works " if got == wanted else "breaks"
    print(f"{flag}  {a} - {b} + {c} = {got} ({score:.2f}, wanted {wanted})")

check('king', 'man', 'woman', ['queen', 'prince', 'princess', 'duke'], 'queen')
check('better', 'good', 'bad', ['worse', 'worst', 'terrible'], 'worse')
check('bigger', 'big', 'small', ['smaller', 'smallest', 'tiny'], 'smaller')
check('happy', 'sad', 'rich', ['poor', 'wealthy', 'money', 'broke'], 'poor')
heading: Word Arithmetic

text:
from genai import similarity
from genai.embed import averaged_similarity

s1 = "The cat sat on the mat"
s2 = "A feline rested on the rug"
s3 = "Python is a programming language"

for name, score in [("averaged words", averaged_similarity), ("transformer", similarity)]:
    related, unrelated = score(s1, s2), score(s1, s3)
    gap = related - unrelated
    print(f"{name:<15} related={related:.3f}  unrelated={unrelated:.3f}  gap={gap:.3f}")
heading: Sentence Embeddings

text:
from genai import semantic_search
corpus = [
    "Neural networks learn patterns from data",
    "Machine learning powers modern AI systems",
    "Deep learning models can recognize images",
    "The chef simmered the tomato sauce for an hour",
    "Fresh basil and garlic make the pasta fragrant",
    "Bakers knead dough to develop gluten",
    "The telescope captured a distant spiral galaxy",
    "Astronauts orbited the Earth for six months",
    "A rocket launched toward the moon at dawn",
]
for doc in semantic_search("artificial intelligence", corpus):
    print(doc)
heading: Applications: Where Embeddings Show Up

text:
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
import numpy as np
from genai import embed

vecs = np.array([embed(d) for d in corpus])
labels = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42,
                n_init='auto').fit_predict(vecs)
for doc, lab in sorted(zip(corpus, labels), key=lambda x: x[1]):
    print(f"[{lab}] {doc}")
heading: Applications: Where Embeddings Show Up

fill  = next(t for t in CODE_TASKS if t["key"] == "gcd")

print("\nFILL IN THE MIDDLE")
print(fill["prefix"] + "____" + fill["suffix"])

from genai.rag import (
    build_wiki_store, wiki_retrieve_dense,
    wiki_retrieve_sparse, wiki_retrieve_hybrid,
    topic_p_at_k, topic_recall_at_k)

wiki_store, wiki_docs = build_wiki_store()
print(f"wiki_store: {len(wiki_store._docs)} chunks "
      f"from {len(wiki_docs)} articles")
for topic in ["Backpropagation", "BERT_(language_model)", "GPT-3"]:
    n = sum(1 for d in wiki_store._docs
            if d['meta']['topic'] == topic)
    print(f"  {topic}: {n} chunks")
heading: A Second Corpus, with Ground Truth

text:
##system
from genai.viz import plot_retrieval_comparison
from genai.rag import WIKI_EVAL_QUERIES as WIKI_EVAL
K_EVAL, ws = 3, wiki_store
labels, dense_s, sparse_s, hybrid_s = [], [], [], []
for label, query, target in WIKI_EVAL:
    labels.append(label)
    d = wiki_retrieve_dense(query, ws, K_EVAL)
    s = wiki_retrieve_sparse(query, kw_score, ws, K_EVAL)
    h = wiki_retrieve_hybrid(query, kw_score, ws, 0.5, K_EVAL)
    dense_s.append(topic_p_at_k(d, target, K_EVAL))
    sparse_s.append(topic_p_at_k(s, target, K_EVAL))
    hybrid_s.append(topic_p_at_k(h, target, K_EVAL))
plot_retrieval_comparison(labels, dense_s, sparse_s, hybrid_s, k=K_EVAL)
heading: A Second Corpus, with Ground Truth

text:
##system
def grounded_rag(question: str, store: DocumentStore,
                 k: int = 3) -> tuple[str, list]:
    sources = store.search(question, k=k)
    context = '\n\n'.join(f'[{i+1}] {d}'
                          for i, d in enumerate(sources))
    prompt  = (
        f'Answer using ONLY the numbered sources below. '
        f'Cite with [1], [2] etc.\n\n{context}\n\n'
        f'Question: {question}')
    return ask(prompt, max_tokens=150, system=None), sources
heading: Grounded Generation and Citation

text:
##system
from genai import show_grounded

answer, sources = grounded_rag(
    'What do temperature and top-k each control when the '
    'model picks its next token?', enriched)
show_grounded(answer, sources)
heading: Grounded Generation and Citation

text:
##system
def hallucination_check(answer: str, docs: list[str]) -> dict:
    context = ' '.join(docs)
    verdict = ask(
        f'Does this answer contain claims not in the context?\n'
        f'Answer: {answer}\nContext: {context[:400]}\n'
        'Reply: GROUNDED or HALLUCINATION, then one sentence.',
        max_tokens=50)
    return {'verdict': verdict, 'answer_length': count_tokens(answer)}
heading: Grounded Generation and Citation

text:
##system
answer, sources = grounded_rag(
    'What temperature did the Codex team find best '
    'when keeping a single completion?', enriched)
show_grounded(answer, sources)
print('\nCheck:', hallucination_check(answer, sources)['verdict'])
heading: Grounded Generation and Citation

    "mood does it convey? Two to three sentences."
)
print(response)
heading: Describing the World

text:
##system
# What `ask_image` really ships to the model: the picture, turned into text.
import base64

img_bytes = open("images/mm/great_wave.jpg", "rb").read()
encoded = base64.b64encode(img_bytes).decode()
message = {"role": "user", "content": "Describe what you see.",
           "images": [encoded]}

print(f"{len(img_bytes):,} bytes of JPEG  →  {len(encoded):,} characters of text")
print("starts with:", encoded[:48])
print("the model receives these fields:", list(message))
heading: Describing the World

text:
##eval
# Score two vision models reading 25 handwritten MNIST digits.
import re
from genai import ask_image, mnist_digits

read_digit = lambda t: (re.findall(r"\d", t) or ["?"])[0]
digits = mnist_digits(n=25, seed=7)
prompt = "What single handwritten digit is shown? Reply with only the digit."
for model in ["gemma4:latest", "llava:latest"]:
    hits = sum(read_digit(ask_image(b, prompt, model=model)) == str(lbl)
               for b, lbl in digits)
    print(f"{model:14} read {hits}/25 correctly ({100 * hits // 25}%)")
heading: Reading Text and Context

text:
##user
# Two script faces, two models: our capable Gemma and the weaker LLaVA.
from genai import ask_image

prompt = "Transcribe the handwriting in this image exactly. Reply with only the words."
for model in ["gemma4:latest", "llava:latest"]:
    for style in ["cursive", "ornate"]:
        img = open(f"images/mm/text_{style}.png", "rb").read()
        reply = ask_image(img, prompt, model=model)
        print(f"{model:14} {style:8} → {reply!r}")

##system
import time
from genai import (
    ask, time_call, time_chat, compare_models, embed, DocumentStore,
    plot_throughput, plot_context_cost, plot_conversation_cache,
    plot_generation_sag, plot_prompt_compression, plot_task_ladder,
    plot_quantization, plot_embed_vs_gen)
from genai.rag import build_wiki_store
heading: Understanding the Performance Metrics

text:
##system
# Grow the prompt, measure how long the model spends reading it.
filler = 'The quick brown fox. ' * 20
rows = []
for n in [1, 5, 10, 20, 40]:
    m = time_call(filler * n + ' What comes next?')
    rows.append({'prompt_tokens': m['prompt_tokens'],
                 'prompt_ms': m['prompt_ms']})
plot_context_cost(rows)
heading: Context Length and Evaluation Time

text:
##system
# Six questions, one study session.
questions = [
    "What's a token in a language model?",
    "How do embeddings relate to tokens?",
    "What is retrieval-augmented generation?",
    "How does an AI agent use tools?",
    "Why do language models hallucinate?",
    "Summarize everything we've covered so far.",
]
heading: Why Conversations Slow Down

text:
##system
# Resend the whole transcript every turn, timing each one.
chat = [{"role": "system", "content": "Answer in one short paragraph."}]
warm, prefixes = [], []
for q in questions:
    chat.append({"role": "user", "content": q})
    prefixes.append(list(chat))
    reply, m = time_chat(chat)
    chat.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    warm.append(m)
heading: Why Conversations Slow Down

text:
##system
# Replay each snapshot against a model that's never seen the chat.
cold = [time_chat(p, fresh=True)[1] for p in prefixes]
plot_conversation_cache(warm, cold)
heading: Why Conversations Slow Down

text:
##system
# Keep the session going until the transcript passes 6,000 tokens.
wide = {"num_ctx": 8192}            # widen Ollama's 4,096-token window
chat = [{"role": "system", "content": "Answer in one short paragraph."}]
speeds = []
for q in questions + ["Tell me more."] * 75:
    chat.append({"role": "user", "content": q})
    reply, m = time_chat(chat, options=wide)
    chat.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    speeds.append(m)
    if m["prompt_tokens"] > 6000:
        break
plot_generation_sag(speeds)
heading: Why Conversations Slow Down

text:
##system
# Same model, three precisions: size, speed, and do the answers still agree?
import requests
variants = ["llama3.2:1b-instruct-q4_K_M",
            "llama3.2:1b-instruct-q8_0",
            "llama3.2:1b-instruct-fp16"]
sizes = {m["name"]: m["size"] / 1e9
         for m in requests.get("http://localhost:11434/api/tags").json()["models"]}
q = "Explain gravity in one sentence."
rows = [{"model": v, "size_gb": round(sizes[v], 2),
         "tps": time_call(q, model=v)["tokens_per_sec"]} for v in variants]
for v in variants:
    print(f"{v.split('-')[-1]:7s} "
          f"{ask(q, model=v, max_tokens=40, options={'temperature': 0})[:100]}...")
plot_quantization(rows)
heading: Quantization: Trading Precision for Speed

    print(f"{effort:7}-> {ex['answer']!r}   {bill} tokens, {secs}s")
